catalog.title: Listen & read · Ears and eyes on realistic turns
catalog.description: Ears and eyes on realistic turns · A2 listen & read in “Work & study”: Dutch you can use the same week, guided steps, and a short check at the end.

pedagogy.objective: By the end of this lesson, you can understand short input about work & study, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions
can_do_outcomes: Describe your job or studies and typical tasks in simple Dutch
Write or say a very short polite email or app message to a colleague
Follow a short exchange in this theme and reuse one chunk.

grammar_points.point: infinitive clauses with om … te for purpose
examples_nl: Ik bel om een afspraak te maken.
We mailen om de deadline te bevestigen.
examples_en: I'm calling to make an appointment.
We're emailing to confirm the deadline.

grammar_points.point: separable verbs in work context (meeting inplannen, doorgeven)
examples_nl: Ik heb het verslag gisteren afgerond.
Ben je op tijd bij de vergadering gekomen?
examples_en: Did you get to the meeting on time?

grammar_points.point: present perfect recognition (ik heb … gedaan) for experience
examples_en: Good morning, I'm working from home today.

micro_outcomes: I can recognise key words about work & study in short Dutch texts or audio.
I can copy at least one useful chunk into a sentence about myself.
I can finish most guided tasks in the lesson without peeking at translations on the second try.
I can pass the quick self-check at the end of the lesson.
I know what to revise tomorrow in two minutes or less.

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Understand short input about work & study, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions

**Grammar thread today:** Purpose with om … te — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Work & study**.

Words you might use today:
• **collega**
• **vergadering**
• **deadline**
• **pauze**
• **dienst**
• **rooster**
• **stage**
• **opleiding**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.example_response: Gisteren had ik een drukke dag op kantoor. Na het werk maakte ik aantekeningen voor een cursus en belde ik een collega kort.

steps.activity:
**Listen & read** this short conversation. Read twice: first for gist, then for details.

**Manager:** Kunnen we de deadline naar vrijdag verschuiven?

**Jij:** Ja, als ik hulp krijg bij het testen.

**Manager:** Dan vraag ik Lisa om mee te kijken. Stuur een korte update in Teams.

**Jij:** Prima. Ik stuur vandaag nog een concept.

**Gist check:** Who are the people, and what is the situation?
**Detail check:** Note one time phrase or place you recognise.

**Today's grammar — *om … te* (purpose)**

**Rule:** *Ik bel **om** een afspraak **te** maken.* Infinitive at the end.

**Patterns:**
• *We mailen om de tijd te bevestigen.* · *Ik kom om een vraag te stellen.*

**Produce:**
1. Why you call the gemeente or GP in one *om … te* sentence.
2. Second sentence with a different verb.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — final -n:** *eten, gaan* — don’t swallow the *-n* completely in careful speech.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**infinitive clauses with om … te for purpose**
• NL: Ik bel om een afspraak te maken. / We mailen om de deadline te bevestigen.
• EN: I'm calling to make an appointment. / We're emailing to confirm the deadline.

**separable verbs in work context (meeting inplannen, doorgeven)**
• NL: Ik heb het verslag gisteren afgerond. / Ben je op tijd bij de vergadering gekomen?
• EN: I finished the report yesterday. / Did you get to the meeting on time?

**present perfect recognition (ik heb … gedaan) for experience**
• NL: Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis. / Met vriendelijke groet, …
• EN: Good morning, I'm working from home today. / Kind regards, …

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.


**Culture tip:** Work emails often start with Goedemorgen/Goedemiddag + name; lunch breaks are commonly around 30 minutes; part-time work is frequent.
*How does this compare with where you live? One sentence in your own words.*

**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **collega** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **vergadering** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **deadline**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **collega** or **vergadering**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"Ik bel om een afspraak te maken." — what does it mean in English (your own words)?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Manager: Kunnen we de deadline naar vrijdag verschuiven?*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Manager: Kunnen we de deadline naar vrijdag verschuiven?*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **collega** and fits *Purpose with om … te*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:
**Phrase bank (use freely)**
• collega
• vergadering
• deadline
• pauze
• dienst
• rooster
• stage
• opleiding
• werkgever
• salaris
• thuiswerken
• inbox

**Your task:** Write or say **4–6 short sentences** in Dutch about this unit theme.
Rules: include **at least one question** and one connector (**en**, **maar**, or **want**).

**Example starter (you can change it):**
Vandaag … Ik wil … **want** … **En jij?**

**Quick self-check**

Try each question, then tap **Check answer**. The correct answer stays hidden until you submit.

**Tip:** Say Dutch options out loud if that helps.

**Afterwards:** If three or more felt easy, open **Quiz** for a short recap. If not, go back to **Guided practice** once more.
steps.teacher_notes: Rendered with interactive UI when interaction is present.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **collega**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag collega met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Listen & read · Names, places & plans in context** — we keep building **Work & study** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

Emails often open with **Goedemorgen** / **Goedemiddag** + the name; closings use *Met vriendelijke groet* or a shorter *Groet* among close colleagues. Subject lines stay concrete.

**Phrases — e-mail & office**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis.* | Good morning, I'm WFH today. |
| *Kunt u het concept vandaag nog sturen?* | Could you send the draft today? |
| *Met vriendelijke groet,* | Kind regards, |

**Mini dialogue**

**A:** Kun je donderdag om 10:00 vergaderen?
**B:** Helaas niet; kan het om 14:00?
**A:** Prima, ik stuur een Teams-uitnodiging.

**Say or write:** Reschedule one meeting in one polite Dutch sentence.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?
steps.teacher_notes: Link to next lesson id in CMS when wired.

quiz_ideas: Self-check: choose the best reply to "Hoe gaat het?" (see step 7 in the lesson).
Gap-fill idea: write a sentence with **collega** in the present tense.
True/false: in Dutch yes/no questions the verb often comes before the subject.
Say one new polite request starting with **Kunt u …** or **Mag ik …**.
Paraphrase one Dutch line from the lesson input in your own English.
assessment.success_criteria: You can complete the step-7 self-check comfortably and produce at least one clear Dutch sentence about the lesson theme (spoken or written).

content_refs.notes: Slow audio assumed for lines marked *slow* in builder CMS.
